title: Bir Aishvan Wildlife Sanctuary
tagline: Bir Aishvan Wildlife Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Punjab

overview.short: Bir Aishwan Wildlife Sanctuary—also locally known as Sohian Bir—is a wildlife sanctuary located approximately 3 km from Sangrur city in Sangrur district, in the Indian state of Pun
overview.description: Bir Aishwan Wildlife Sanctuary—also locally known as Sohian Bir—is a wildlife sanctuary located approximately 3 km from Sangrur city in Sangrur district, in the Indian state of Punjab. It was declared as a sanctuary on 28 February 1952. It covers an area of 264.40 hectares (≈ 2.64 km²).

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Sangrur
topPlaces.description: Sangrur is a city in the Indian state of Punjab, India. It is the headquarters of Sangrur district.

topPlaces.name: Bahadurpur, Sangrur
topPlaces.description: Bahadurpur is a village in the Sangrur District of the Indian state of Punjab. It is located at 4 km from the holy site of Mastuana Sahib, 10 km from Sangrur, 32 km from Barnala, on Sangrur-Barnala main road.

topPlaces.name: Badrukhan
topPlaces.description: Badrukhan is a big village about 5 km from Sangrur, the district headquarters, on Sangrur-Barnala road in Punjab, India.

topPlaces.name: Khurana, India
topPlaces.description: Khurana is a village in the Sangrur district of Punjab, India. It's located seven kilometers east of Sangrur city, on the Sangrur-Patiala road.

items.note: Sangrur is a city in the Indian state of Punjab, India. It is the headquarters of Sangrur district.

Bahadurpur is a village in the Sangrur District of the Indian state of Punjab. It is located at 4 km from the holy site of Mastuana Sahib, 10 km from Sangrur, 32 km from Barnala, on Sangrur-Barnala main road.
Badrukhan is a big village about 5 km from Sangrur, the district headquarters, on Sangrur-Barnala road in Punjab, India.
Khurana is a village in the Sangrur district of Punjab, India. It's located seven kilometers east of Sangrur city, on the Sangrur-Patiala road.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Bir Aishvan Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Bir Aishvan Wildlife Sanctuary is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Bir Aishvan Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Chandigarh International Airport (103 km) and the nearest railway station is Ludhiana Junction (75 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Bir Aishvan Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Bir Aishvan Wildlife Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Bir Aishvan Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Bir Aishvan Wildlife Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Bir Aishvan Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are Sangrur, Bahadurpur, Sangrur, Badrukhan, Khurana, India.
